laws.title: Public Law 1-1 | Enactment of the U.S. Codes Act
laws.status_basis: Foundational enactment establishing the Code baseline; nothing in the source text or current record shows a wholesale repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Adopts the act's short title and states the Code-enactment purpose.
provisions.exact_change: Baseline note only; no discrete section target exists.
provisions.evidence: this act may be cited as the Enactment of the U.S. Codes Act

provisions.text_summary: Explains what the United States Code is and how it is published.
provisions.evidence: The United States Code is the codification by subject matter

provisions.text_summary: Lists the title structure of the Code as adopted by the act.
provisions.exact_change: Structural baseline for the title list, not a section-level amendment.
provisions.evidence: TITLE 1 - GENERAL PROVISIONS ... TITLE 54 - NATIONAL PARK SERVICE AND RELATED PROGRAMS

provisions.text_summary: Makes the enactment immediately effective.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall take effect immediately after the passage of the Congress

current_implementation.detail: The current note carries a full-text quotedContent dump and a Trello link; keep only a concise baseline note.

recommended_actions: Keep a concise foundational note at /us/usc/t1/s1.
Remove the full-text quotedContent dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 1-2 | United States Bar Act
laws.status_basis: The source contains a live substantive rule for Attorney General control of the bar exam and no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the United States Bar Act

provisions.text_summary: States the act's purpose: give the Attorney General full control over the bar exam.
provisions.evidence: Give the Attorney General full control over the United States Bar Exam

provisions.text_summary: Defines the bar examination.
provisions.exact_change: Create a standalone note or general provision for the bar-exam definition; no existing section target is cited.
provisions.evidence: The bar examination (exam) shall be defined as an examination to assess one's competency

provisions.text_summary: Gives the Attorney General control over the exam content and administration.
provisions.exact_change: Standalone general rule should be anchored near Title 28 or Title 5.
provisions.evidence: The Attorney General of the United States will have full control over The United States Bar Exam

provisions.text_summary: Effective on passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be enacted once passed through both chambers of Congress and signed by the President

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is at Title 3 and declares the law repealed/superseded; the source supports a current substantive rule, and the note should be relocated to a judiciary/DOJ anchor.

recommended_actions: Correct the status to current.
Move the note from /us/usc/t3/s1 to a Title 28 or Title 5 anchor.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 1-3 | Closing Cabinet Doors Act
laws.status_basis: The act permanently restructures executive departments and contains no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and external definition link.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only a concise historical note; the Google Drive definition is not codifiable text.
provisions.evidence: This act may be cited as the CCD Act ... United States Code shall be defined as; https://drive.google.com

provisions.text_summary: Closes the named departments and transfers Veterans Affairs under Defense.
provisions.exact_change: Record the department closures and the VA transfer in a concise note; no section-specific strike text is determinable.
provisions.evidence: Department of Labor shall be closed in its entirety ... It shall be relocated under the Department of Defense

provisions.text_summary: Blanket repeal of all Code provisions involving the listed departments.
provisions.exact_change: Blanket repeal is not reducible to specific strikes without editorial judgment.
provisions.evidence: All codes within the United States Code that involve all Departments ... shall be repealed in its entirety

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: This bill will take effect immediately after passage

current_implementation.detail: The current note does not carry the real department-closure consequences into specific Code text and includes a full-text dump plus Trello link.

recommended_actions: Keep a concise historical/structural note at /us/usc/t5.
Do not invent section-level strikes for the blanket repeal clause.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 1-4 | Retracting of Resignations Act
laws.status_basis: The act creates a permanent rule about retracting resignations; the source defect affects codification, not the law's existence.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, conflicting-legislation clause, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Retracting Resignations ... shall go into effect immediately upon passage

provisions.text_summary: Makes immediate resignations non-retractable and future-effective resignations retractable.
provisions.exact_change: Create new 18 U.S.C. 918 with the immediate-vs-future resignation rule stated in section 2(a)-(b).
provisions.evidence: Retracting resignations when the effective date is immediate shall be unlawful

provisions.text_summary: Directs codification in Title 18, Chapter 43, section 918, but cites a nonexistent section 3(a).
provisions.exact_change: Record the codification instruction and preserve the source defect.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(a) of this Act shall be codified under Chapter 43 of Title 18 ... under Section 918

current_implementation.detail: The XML only adds a note at Title 18 section 1; the act itself points to a new section 918 and the source contains an internal section-numbering defect.

recommended_actions: If the enactment is to be implemented, create 18 U.S.C. 918 from section 2(a)-(b).
Preserve the section 2(c) typo as a source defect.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-5 | Judicial Efficiency Act (Keywords: Maximum, Minimum, Sentence, Sentencing)
laws.status_basis: Public Law 19-149 expressly repealed this act in full and replaced its sentencing scheme.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, effective date, conflict clause, and severability.
provisions.evidence: This piece of legislation shall be referred to as the Judicial Efficiency Act

provisions.text_summary: Sets short imprisonment ceilings for listed Title 18 offenses and chapter groups.
provisions.exact_change: Superseded by the later replacement sentencing regime in PL 19-149.
provisions.evidence: The maximum imprisonment time for U.S. Code 1111 Murder shall not exceed seven days

provisions.text_summary: Caps accumulated sentences at thirty days and limits judicial sentencing.
provisions.exact_change: Superseded by the later replacement sentencing regime in PL 19-149.
provisions.evidence: No Judge may issue a Judgement that constitute a sentence longer than thirty days

current_implementation.detail: The note correctly preserves history for a repealed act, but it still carries a full-text dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise historical note for PL 2-5.
Do not restore the superseded sentencing ceilings into current text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-6 | Shelby-Kabob Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: Public Law 14-89 later repealed this act in full and enacted replacement misconduct rules.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and general provisions.
provisions.evidence: This piece of legislation shall be referred to as the Shelby-Kabob Act of 2021

provisions.text_summary: Creates the unlawful discharge of a taser offense.
provisions.evidence: whoever discharges his or her taser ... without probable cause

provisions.text_summary: Creates the federal-agent abuse-of-power offense.
provisions.evidence: Whoever on duty as a federal law enforcement officer ... shall be fined

provisions.text_summary: Directs codification of sections II and III in Title 18.
provisions.exact_change: The codification instruction is historical only because the law was later repealed.
provisions.evidence: Section II of this Act shall be codified under Chapter 7 of Title 18 ... Section 120

current_implementation.detail: The note reflects repeal history, but the XML still contains a dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise repeal-history note for PL 2-6.
Do not revive the superseded Title 18 offenses.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-7 | Court of Appeals Temporary Suspension Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: The act is expressly superseded and repealed in full by later law PL 3-24.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, reasoning, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Court of Appeals Temporary Suspension Act of 2021

provisions.text_summary: Temporarily closes the Court of Appeals and transfers records to the Supreme Court.
provisions.evidence: shall be temporarily closed ... all records ... shall be moved to the United States Supreme Court

provisions.text_summary: Allows the Chief Justice to lift the suspension under stated conditions and then abolishes those powers.
provisions.evidence: the Chief Justice shall be empowered to lift the suspension

current_implementation.detail: The note preserves the repealed history, but it still carries a full-text dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise historical note for PL 2-7.
Do not treat the temporary suspension as current law.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-8 | National Security Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: The act creates a continuing national-security framework and nothing in the current record shows a wholesale repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, effective date, conflict clause, and severability.
provisions.evidence: This piece of legislation shall be referred to as the National Federal Torts Act

provisions.text_summary: Establishes a National Security Council under the Executive Office of the President.
provisions.exact_change: Create operative Title 50 text for the National Security Council.
provisions.evidence: There shall be a National Security Council

provisions.text_summary: Sets Council membership categories and appointments.
provisions.evidence: The Voting Members of the Principals Committee shall be

provisions.text_summary: Defines the Council's responsibilities and binding policy role.
provisions.evidence: To assess and appraise the objectives, commitments, and risks to the United States

provisions.text_summary: Details chair powers, reporting, declassification, and related controls.
provisions.evidence: The Chair of the Council shall

provisions.text_summary: Creates the national-security-threat designation regime.
provisions.evidence: may be declared as a National Security Threat

provisions.text_summary: Creates the transitional-threat regime and related presidential authority.
provisions.evidence: The Council shall additionally be tasked with coordinating and directing the activities

current_implementation.detail: The current XML keeps this major permanent framework as a note only; it needs real Title 50 text, not just note treatment.

recommended_actions: Implement the National Security Council framework as operative Title 50 text.
Keep any note concise and remove the full-text dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-9 | District of Columbia City Charter
laws.status_basis: The charter is a live local governance instrument and nothing in the current record shows a wholesale repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the municipality and sets general powers and rights.
provisions.exact_change: Retain as a local-law note rather than permanent positive-law text.
provisions.evidence: A political subdivision is hereby created within the United States to be known as The City of Washington, District of Columbia

provisions.text_summary: Defines the legislative branch and council structure.
provisions.evidence: The City Council comprises the Legislative Branch of the city government

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the administrative branch and mayoral powers.
provisions.evidence: The Administrative Branch of the City Government for Washington, District of Columbia is hereby created

provisions.text_summary: Sets Metropolitan Police and Fire Department authority.
provisions.evidence: The District of Columbia shall have a Police Department

provisions.text_summary: Creates the District Attorney's office and its functions.
provisions.evidence: The District Attorney of the District of Columbia shall be the officer in full charge and control

current_implementation.detail: The statutory-note approach is correct for a local charter, but the Trello link should be removed.

recommended_actions: Keep the charter as a concise local-law statutory note.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-10 | Amendment to the District of Columbia City Charter
laws.status_basis: This is a live amendment to the local charter and nothing in the record shows a wholesale repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, effective date, conflict clause, and severability.
provisions.evidence: This piece of legislation shall be referred to as the Amendment to the District of Columbia City Charter

provisions.text_summary: Changes the mayoral and council terms and partially repeals/amends the charter articles.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the charter's mayoral term to 90 days and council term to 45 days, and apply the stated partial repeal/amendment to Article III 3.02(a) and Article II 2.01(A(a)(i)).
provisions.evidence: The Mayoral Term shall last a total of ninety (90) Days

current_implementation.detail: The act expressly amends charter text, but the current repository only has a note at the local-law anchor.

recommended_actions: Carry the term changes into the charter text or the proper local-law apparatus.
Remove the Trello link.
